title: Built an automated bot that trades Kalshi prediction markets 24/7 — here’s the live dashboard
author: Character-Wonder-422
selftext:
been trading on Kalshi for a while and got frustrated trying to manually monitor dozens of markets at once across NBA, MLB, NHL, Soccer and Tennis simultaneously.

So I built a bot to do it automatically.

How it works:

The bot pulls live game data from ESPN every 15 seconds and compares real win probabilities to Kalshi’s market prices. When it finds a market where the odds are off by 5%+ from what the data says — it places the trade automatically. No manual input.

It covers NBA, MLB, NHL, Soccer and Tennis all at once. While games are live it’s constantly scanning for mispricings and firing when it finds edge.

What I built on top of it:

Turned it into a SaaS so other people can run it on their own Kalshi accounts. You connect your own API key, set your risk profile, and the bot runs 24/7 on your account. You keep 100% of whatever you make.

Live dashboard shows every trade in real time — market, side, entry price, P&L, win rate.

Pricing: $79/mo Starter · $149/mo Pro · $249/mo Elite

Link: kalshibotpro.com

Happy to answer any questions about how the strategy works or the tech behind it.

Not financial advice — prediction market trading involves real risk and you can lose money.

comments.author: Limp_Statistician529
comments.body:
Can this be applied to other platforms as well? 

Curious to see it behind the scenes

replies.author: Character-Wonder-422
replies.body: Absolutely could be. Id just have to intergrade the other platforms.  What platform you thinking?

replies.author: Limp_Statistician529
replies.body:
Interestinggg, I saw an early one which is OmenX but not sure it it will work,

  
Would love to see how you'll work on that platform

replies.author: Character-Wonder-422
replies.body:
OmenX is interesting — prediction market space is definitely growing fast.
KalshiBot Pro is built specifically for Kalshi right now because it’s CFTC regulated which makes it legal in all 50 states — that’s a big deal for US traders. The strategy works because Kalshi’s odds are set by retail traders, not sharp books, so mispricings are consistent and findable.
Expanding to other platforms is definitely on the roadmap. The core engine — live data comparison, edge detection, automated order placement — could work on any prediction market with a decent API. Kalshi just made the most sense to start since it has the most sports markets and liquidity.
Would be curious what draws you to OmenX specifically — is it the decentralized aspect?

comments.author: Opening-Berry-6041
comments.body: Dude, pulling live ESPN data every 15 seconds and comparing those win probabilities to Kalshi's prices sounds like a legit feat of engineering and algorithmic thinking, seriously how did you even start visualizing that whole process?

replies.author: Character-Wonder-422
replies.body:
Honestly it started way simpler than it sounds now. I knew Kalshi’s markets were set by retail traders not sharp books so there had to be mispricings — I just needed a way to find them faster than any human could manually.
The ESPN scoreboard API is actually public and free which was the lucky break. Once I had live scores and time remaining I could calculate a real win probability using a model that accounts for score differential, sport, and how much time is left. Compare that to what Kalshi is pricing the market at and the edge basically pops out.
The hard part wasn’t the math — it was making the whole pipeline reliable. Kalshi’s API authentication uses RSA-PSS signing, the order placement has to be fast enough to catch mispricings before they close, and managing multiple markets across 5 sports simultaneously without the thing falling over took a lot of iteration.
Went through probably a dozen different versions before it was stable enough to run live.
Now it’s running 24/7 on Railway scanning everything automatically. Genuinely one of the most satisfying things I’ve built.
Happy to get into more technical detail if you’re into that kind of thing — and there’s a free trial going on right now if you want to see it trade live.

comments.author: pr0XYTV
comments.body: oh cool! i built something like this too i didnt think to charge for it as a website not a bad idea

comments.author: Character-Wonder-422
comments.body: Update — still have free trial spots available. If you commented and haven’t DM’d me yet, shoot me a message and I’ll get you set up today. Takes about 5 minutes to connect your Kalshi account. No credit card, no commitment.

replies.author: Character-Wonder-422
replies.body:
Honest answer — it depends on the day and what sports are in season. Some days it finds a lot of edges, some days fewer.
What I can tell you is the strategy is mathematically sound — it’s not picking winners, it’s finding markets where Kalshi’s price is statistically off from what the live game data says. That’s a real edge that exists because retail traders set the prices, not sharp books.
I’m not going to throw out specific return numbers because I don’t want to oversell it — prediction market trading has real risk and results vary based on starting bankroll, market conditions, and which sports are active.
That’s exactly why I’m offering free 1-week trials — I’d rather you see it trade live on your own account than take my word for any numbers. DM me and I’ll get you set up today, no credit card required.
